name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart is an Ohio financial services retailer offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay, and access to third-party short-term loan products in-store.
description_long:
CheckSmart is a multi-location financial services retailer operating exclusively in Ohio. The Columbus location at 2496 Morse Rd sits at the northeast corner of Morse Rd and Cleveland Ave and is one of at least five CheckSmart branches in the Columbus metro area. The broader network spans additional Ohio cities including Heath, Marion, Marysville, Reynoldsburg, Springfield, and Whitehall, making it a recognizable regional chain for consumers who rely on non-bank financial services.

The store's core in-store offerings include check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment. Beyond these staples, customers can obtain a Green Dot® Visa® Debit Card, exchange unused gift cards for cash, and access short-term loan products. The loan products are facilitated through an unaffiliated third-party lender — CheckSmart is not itself the lender but serves as an access point. To prepare for a store visit, applicants are asked to bring a valid government-issued ID, proof of employment or income, a working phone number, a valid email address, and proof of address.

CheckSmart differentiates itself operationally through online appointment scheduling, which the company promotes as a way to streamline the in-store process and reduce wait times. The store is open seven days a week, with extended Friday hours running from 8 AM to 8 PM — the widest window of any day. Saturday closes early at 4 PM and Sunday is limited to 11 AM–5 PM, so weekend availability is reduced compared to weekdays.

CheckSmart is a practical option for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate access to financial transaction services without a traditional bank account. However, the website discloses no fee schedules or loan rates, and because loan products come from an unaffiliated third party, customers should carefully review loan terms directly with the lending partner before proceeding. As with most check-cashing operations, costs for individual services are best confirmed in-store prior to committing.
pros: Seven-day-a-week operation with extended Friday hours (8 AM–8 PM)
Online appointment scheduling reduces in-store wait times
Green Dot® Visa® Debit Card available for purchase in-store
Gift card-to-cash exchange offered as an additional liquidity option
Regional Ohio network with multiple Columbus-area locations for accessibility
Wire transfers available alongside check cashing for broader remittance needs
Bill pay service consolidates multiple financial tasks in one visit
cons: Loan products are from an unaffiliated third-party lender — CheckSmart is not the direct lender, adding a layer of separation from loan terms
No fee schedules or loan rates published on the website, requiring an in-store visit to understand costs
Saturday hours end at 4 PM and Sunday hours are only 11 AM–5 PM, limiting weekend access
Service footprint is limited to Ohio — no availability for out-of-state customers
Short-term lending products at check-cashing outlets typically carry high APRs compared to mainstream alternatives
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need check cashing without a bank account
Ohio residents seeking short-term loan access through a retail storefront
Consumers needing money orders, wire transfers, or bill pay in a single location
People looking to convert unused gift cards into cash quickly
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best suited for Ohio consumers who are unbanked or underbanked and need immediate access to check cashing, money orders, or basic payment services without a traditional bank account. The main caveat is that loan products are not issued by CheckSmart directly — they come from a third-party lender whose rates and terms are not disclosed online — so borrowers should review loan agreements carefully before signing.
services: Green Dot® Visa® Debit Card (in-store purchase)
Short-Term Loan Products (via unaffiliated third-party lender)
